hawkish_phrases: some data had suggested a firming of underlying inflation trends
postponing the removal of accommodation could increase financial distortions
federal funds rate is likely to remain, for some time, below levels that are expected to prevail in the longer run
Inflation picked up in recent months
dovish_phrases: global economic and financial developments continue to pose risks
inflation continued to run below the Committee's 2 percent longer-run objective
market-based measures of inflation compensation remain low
Several members expressed concern that longer-run inflation expectations may have declined
many participants expressed the view that a somewhat lower path for the federal funds rate than they had projected in December now seemed most likely
summary: The Committee voted 9-1 to hold the federal funds rate at 1/4 to 1/2 percent with George dissenting in favor of a 25bp hike, maintaining a cautious stance amid global risks and inflation below target despite labor market strength.
